known_target.goal: identify one byte-frozen computation recipe that reproduces the remembered negative association

claim_boundary.full_rated479_search: historical specification recovery on the same dataset
claim_boundary.clean_replay: computational reproduction on the same frozen data

units.candidate_order_semantics: opaque protobuf source order; never a predictor and never interpreted as rating rank

orchestration_contract.lead_model: frontier reasoning model owns the frozen registry, gate decisions, aggregate interpretation, and stop/reopen decisions
orchestration_contract.execution_workers: lower-cost workers receive immutable cell batches, may compute only registered artifacts, and cannot edit axes, joins, ranking, thresholds, or prior ledger rows
orchestration_contract.independent_validator: fresh high-reasoning agent reviews exact hashes and reimplements only the selected recipe in a clean worktree
orchestration_contract.worker_output_visibility: workers return receipts and artifacts; no worker sees a partial leaderboard or row-level ratings outside the authorized join operation
orchestration_contract.shared_failure_rule: any worker deviation, source drift, or schema mismatch terminates the batch and cannot be repaired by silently changing the contract

rating_blind_feature_bank.source: canonical score-stripped WOD bundle plus the frozen checksum-bound RQ009 M3 artifact set

frozen_envelope.support_semantics: score every otherwise eligible WOD row; support_gate_pass and ood_abstain are diagnostics only and never mask, drop, or invalidate a row
frozen_envelope.wod_transfer_semantics: PI-acknowledged out-of-support extrapolation; historical bounded pilot recorded 0/228 in-support
frozen_envelope.derivative_authority: reports/plans/RQ014_envelope_builder_contract_v2.json#/timeline_and_state_contract remains the exact-window-local state authority only; its InterHub builder and envelope-axis clauses are superseded

fixed_estimator.wod_focal_reference: scene-level legacy_route_reference_v1 from the envelope builder contract, shared byte-identically by all three candidates
fixed_estimator.wod_counterpart_reference: observed counterpart XY on the exact resampled estimator-window grid
fixed_estimator.reference_preparation: the pinned estimator applies pinned smooth_ployline exactly once to each raw reference array
fixed_estimator.local_window_output: terminal estimator row at the window terminal physical time

sampling_axis.candidate_rule: exported native effective timeline after frozen point-zero duplicate removal
sampling_axis.history_and_counterpart_rule: linear interpolation inside observed support to the full tstar-anchored 0.25 s grid; the candidate future remains at its native effective +0.25 s ticks
sampling_axis.operation_order: duplicate decision on raw 4 Hz candidate, then common-support assembly
sampling_axis.derived_state_rule: resample positions only, slice each registered exact closed position window, then reconstruct that window's velocity, acceleration, and heading solely from positions inside it using the envelope builder contract; source candidate dynamics, derivative halos, and state reuse from any larger branch are forbidden

sampling_axis.candidate_rule: after frozen raw point-zero duplicate removal, linearly interpolate XY by absolute timestamp inside observed support
sampling_axis.history_and_counterpart_rule: linear interpolation by absolute timestamp to the same 10 Hz grid
sampling_axis.grid_phase: tstar-anchored integer multiples of 0.1 s; interpolate 0<t<0.25 across the exact history t=0 to first effective candidate t=0.25 seam
sampling_axis.derived_state_rule: interpolate positions only on the tstar-anchored grid, slice each registered exact closed position window, then reconstruct that window's velocity, acceleration, and heading solely from its own positions using the envelope builder contract; never interpolate IPV, use a derivative halo, or reuse state derived for another window

temporal_axis.position_and_state_support: for each exact closed recipe interval slice resampled positions first and derive velocity, acceleration, and heading only inside that window; CH and TP consume no state position after tau, and LF/HF/TF consume no state position outside their declared interval

recipes.note: preserves the plausible legacy expanding-future-prefix implementation

recipes.interval: [0,H_common] for every registered finite tau anchor
recipes.note: preserves the plausible legacy one-IPV-per-full-planned-trajectory implementation; the IPV window is tau-invariant but tau anchors remain bounded by the horizon contract

feature_family_enumeration.rule: Cartesian product of sampling_axis and temporal_axis.recipes
feature_family_enumeration.order: sampling_axis order, then temporal recipes order
feature_family_enumeration.machine_enumeration: this file's sampling_axis Cartesian-product temporal_axis.recipes in declared order
feature_family_enumeration.technical_ineligibility: retain the row and terminal reason; never delete a family

horizon_axis.candidate_tau_ticks: every integer tau_tick from rate_hz through 2*rate_hz inclusive, corresponding exactly to tau=1.0,1.0+dt,...,2.0
horizon_axis.anchor_rule: retain the full candidate_tau_ticks set only when every tau_tick<=h_common_tick and every tick's exact registered temporal window is complete inside observed position support; no H20 boundary shortening or partial anchor set is allowed

horizon_axis.candidate_tau_ticks: finite integer closed interval rate_hz,rate_hz+1,...,h_common_tick; empty when h_common_tick<rate_hz
horizon_axis.anchor_rule: from candidate_tau_ticks retain only tau ticks whose exact registered temporal position window is complete inside four-way observed support
horizon_axis.minimum_requirement: the H20 anchor set is complete
horizon_axis.maximum_tau_tick: h_common_tick for every temporal recipe, including TF
horizon_axis.TF_rule: TF uses the same [0,H_common] position window at every retained tau, but its HFEAS tau anchors are still exactly rate_hz..h_common_tick and therefore finite

scene_anchor_domain_binding.execution_rule: WOD feature evaluation and all per-scene readouts consume only the checksum-verified rows for their exact group key; an AVAILABLE group uses exactly its integer tau memberships, requires path_type_or_NA set cardinality 1 equal to that segment's checksum-bound WOD path lookup, and contributes only to that one path branch; a terminal group emits no feature/readout value
scene_anchor_domain_binding.aggregate_separation: no aggregate envelope-query artifact exists in v3; no downstream consumer may reconstruct or share a scene's tau list

pointwise_deviation_axis.NEX: zero inside [L,U]; below L use (L-v)/(M-L); above U use (v-U)/(U-M)
pointwise_deviation_axis.NMD: normalized absolute distance from median M using the side-specific half width

readout_rules.ANCHOR_DOMAIN: for the exact segment_id x feature_id x horizon_id consume only its AVAILABLE integer tau_tick rows from checksum-bound wod_scene_anchor_domain.csv; no readout regenerates, shares, extends, or shortens a scene membership
readout_rules.MEAN: physical-time trapezoid integral divided by common duration
readout_rules.LAST: value at the maximum retained valid tau_tick; for HFEAS this is always <=h_common_tick, including TF
readout_rules.FOUT: physical-time trapezoid-weighted fraction with NEX greater than zero

predictor_cell_enumeration.rule: 16 feature families x 2 horizons x 10 readouts
predictor_cell_enumeration.feature_execution_rule: each cell uses the one frozen M3 artifact set and its exact feature-family/horizon anchor execution; the envelope is not an axis

hard_negative_control.current_role: rating-blind G2R structural candidate/future-leakage fixture and fatal gate only
hard_negative_control.source_interval: closed [-W,0] observed shared ego history and observed counterpart history only
hard_negative_control.branch_isolation: construct one history-only position branch before candidate forking; the function signature accepts no candidate ID, candidate geometry, candidate future, ego future, H_common, envelope result, rating, or rating-derived input
hard_negative_control.state_derivation: for each registered W separately slice the exact closed [-W,0] shared-history position window, then derive velocity, acceleration, and heading only within that window using the same window-local operator; derivative halos and reuse of state from a larger history or candidate branch are forbidden
hard_negative_control.focal_reference: legacy_route_reference_v1 may use only shared past XY and scene intent; it is built once before candidate forking
hard_negative_control.counterpart_reference: observed/resampled counterpart history XY on [-W,0] only
hard_negative_control.candidate_materialization: after the single control value and its canonical byte payload are complete, copy the same payload hash to candidate ordinals 1,2,3 without recomputation
hard_negative_control.future_perturbation_fixture: replace each candidate future and ego future with three distinct finite adversarial trajectories, rerun the isolated control builder, and require exact equality of state bytes, reference bytes, IPV bytes, terminal status, and payload SHA-256 to the unperturbed build
hard_negative_control.expected: candidate ordinals 1,2,3 have byte-identical control payloads before and after every registered future perturbation
hard_negative_control.fixture_and_hash_gate: history-only builder implementation SHA-256, five fixture input/output SHA-256 pairs, pinned estimator SHA-256, Python executable SHA-256, and environment manifest SHA-256 must all match before G2R

prerequisites: all rating-blind feature cells, masks, the frozen M3 envelope, and their hashes are frozen
the 320-row predictor manifest is terminal and immutable
a separately authorized full-rated479 rating access receipt exists
full_data_recovery_screen.split: none; use all rating-complete rated479 scenes for specification recovery

association_support_contract.scene_universe: the frozen 479-row blind scene manifest; no scene is removed because of a rating value
shared_scene_inclusion_predicate: predictor cell is rating-blind AVAILABLE for all three source-order candidates in the scene
exactly three finite joined human scores are present and geometry keys match exactly
all three candidate deviations are finite
the three human scores contain at least two distinct exact binary64 values
the three deviations contain at least two distinct exact binary64 values
association_support_contract.shared_across_associations: RWS, PSP, and PPR for one predictor cell use exactly the same included scenes and the same 3*n candidate rows
association_support_contract.within_cell_support_id: SHA256 over UTF-8 bytes of segment IDs sorted by raw UTF-8 byte order, each followed by exactly one LF byte including the final ID; no BOM, CR, blank line, or omitted terminal LF
association_support_contract.between_cell_support: may differ across predictor cells only because the cell is not rating-blind AVAILABLE for all three candidates in a scene, one or more of its three deviations is nonfinite, or its exact three-deviation vector is constant; rating-key presence, finite-rating, and nonconstant-rating predicates are cell-invariant after the single join and are applied identically to every cell-specific survivor; support_id and all denominator fields are mandatory
association_support_contract.ranking_support: native within-cell shared support; this is an operational historical-recovery ordering, not an equal-cohort causal comparison
association_support_contract.scenario_cluster_missing_rule: the exact CSV missing token NA is not a scenario cluster, is never LOCO-eligible, and remains in the native association support; record its included-scene count in scenario_cluster_na_scene_count_metric

common_support_sensitivity.role: required non-ranking, non-gating sensitivity only; it never changes recovery_compatible, rank, freeze, replay, or verdict
common_support_sensitivity.cell_universe: all 320 registered predictor cell_ids in canonical predictor enumeration order; no globally or scene-locally unavailable cell is skipped
common_support_sensitivity.blind_scene_predicate: a frozen universe scene is included only if every one of the 320 cells is rating-blind AVAILABLE for all three candidates, all three deviations are finite, and the exact three-deviation vector is nonconstant

blind_artifact.encoding: CPython 3.9 csv.writer with dialect=excel, quoting=csv.QUOTE_MINIMAL, lineterminator='\n', UTF-8 without BOM
blind_artifact.support_id: the within_cell_support_id algorithm over artifact data rows; use SHA256 of zero bytes when empty

common_support_sensitivity.post_join_predicate: from the blind artifact retain a scene only when its three geometry-keyed ratings are present and finite and its exact three-rating vector is nonconstant; no predictor-cell-specific predicate is reevaluated

result_artifact.serialization: CPython 3.9 json.dumps with sort_keys=True,separators=(',',':'),ensure_ascii=False,allow_nan=False plus exactly one terminal LF, UTF-8 without BOM
result_artifact.support_id: within_cell_support_id over post_join_predicate survivors in raw UTF-8 order; SHA256 of zero bytes when rated_informative_scene_count=0

field_rules.association_value: FINITE_FLOAT only for a COMMON_SUPPORT_AVAILABLE row and NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED) otherwise
field_rules.n_informative_scenes_metric: FINITE_INT equal to rated_informative_scene_count when it is positive, and NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED) when it is zero

terminal_policy.COMMON_SUPPORT_AVAILABLE: rated_informative_scene_count>0 and all three registered association kernels return finite values
terminal_policy.COMMON_SUPPORT_EMPTY_BLIND: blind_scene_count=0; do not evaluate rating values or associations; all three rows use NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED)
terminal_policy.COMMON_SUPPORT_EMPTY_AFTER_RATING: blind_scene_count>0 but rated_informative_scene_count=0; all three rows use NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED)
terminal_policy.COMMON_SUPPORT_ASSOCIATION_CONSTANT: at least one registered kernel returns ASSOCIATION_CONSTANT; affected association_value is NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED)
terminal_policy.COMMON_SUPPORT_NUMERICAL_FAILURE: at least one registered kernel returns ASSOCIATION_NUMERICAL_FAILURE; affected association_value is NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED)
terminal_policy.row_status_rule: each row records COMMON_SUPPORT_AVAILABLE only for a finite association; otherwise it records the applicable common-support terminal status; top-level status is the first non-AVAILABLE status in row_order, or COMMON_SUPPORT_AVAILABLE when all three are available

association_support_contract.scene_weight: when n_informative_scenes>0, 1/n_informative_scenes; no scene weights are instantiated when it is 0
association_support_contract.candidate_weight: when n_informative_scenes>0, 1/(3*n_informative_scenes); exactly three candidates make pooled PSP/PPR scene-equal; no candidate weights are instantiated when it is 0
association_support_contract.candidate_order: opaque source order used only to align rating and deviation; never a covariate or rank
association_support_contract.tie_rule: average midranks over exact binary64 equality; no tolerance, jitter, ordinal tie-break, or random tie-break
association_support_contract.signed_zero_rule: normalize -0.0 to +0.0 before serialization and ranking

denominator_field_types.typed_metric_namespace: typed leaderboard metric objects are separate fields under stability.row_metric_fields and never replace or alias a raw denominator field

derived_denominator_definitions.n_informative_scenes: N_I as a bare nonnegative JSON integer
derived_denominator_definitions.n_informative_candidates: 3*N_I as a bare nonnegative JSON integer
derived_denominator_definitions.n_excluded_nonfinite_scenes: count of scenes whose mutually exclusive first-failure upstream_status is INELIGIBLE_STATE_NONFINITE, INELIGIBLE_IPV_NUMERICAL, or RATING_VALUE_NONFINITE
derived_denominator_definitions.n_excluded_constant_rating_scenes: count of scenes whose mutually exclusive first-failure upstream_status is RATING_VECTOR_CONSTANT
derived_denominator_definitions.n_excluded_constant_deviation_scenes: count of scenes whose mutually exclusive first-failure upstream_status is DEVIATION_VECTOR_CONSTANT
derived_denominator_definitions.rating_distinct_count_pooled: number of distinct normalized exact binary64 rating values over the 3*N_I canonical informative candidate rows; 0 when N_I=0
derived_denominator_definitions.deviation_distinct_count_pooled: number of distinct normalized exact binary64 deviation values over the 3*N_I canonical informative candidate rows; 0 when N_I=0
derived_denominator_definitions.scene_weight_sum: math.fsum of N_I scene weights in canonical scene order when N_I>0, otherwise 0.0
derived_denominator_definitions.candidate_weight_sum: math.fsum of 3*N_I candidate weights in canonical row order when N_I>0, otherwise 0.0
derived_denominator_definitions.support_id: within_cell_support_id digest over the N_I canonical segment IDs; when N_I=0 use SHA256 of the zero-byte payload, e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

denominator_identities: if N_I>0 then scene_weight_sum=1 within absolute tolerance 1e-12 else scene_weight_sum=0 exactly
if N_I>0 then candidate_weight_sum=1 within absolute tolerance 1e-12 else candidate_weight_sum=0 exactly

stages.B: blind base: every one of the 479 frozen segment IDs is present exactly once and has structural candidate slots 1,2,3; a scene without candidate geometry retains three unavailable slots and fails K rather than disappearing
stages.K: key complete: all three (segment_id,tstar_context_step,candidate_ordinal,geometry_sha256) keys are finite, unique on both sides, and match one-to-one
stages.F: feature complete: the predictor cell's upstream envelope/timeline/path/reference execution is AVAILABLE and all three candidate deviations are finite
stages.R: rating complete: exactly one authorized finite human score joins to each of candidate ordinals 1,2,3
stages.D: distinct: the three exact binary64 ratings and the three exact binary64 deviations each contain at least two distinct values
stages.I: informative invariant: exact row count, deterministic order, weight sums, support_id, and statistic implementation/environment hash gates all pass

ordered_attrition_contract.candidate_order_within_scene: candidate_ordinal integer ascending exactly 1,2,3; candidate_id must equal C1,C2,C3 respectively
ordered_attrition_contract.admission: a scene advances only when all three candidates pass the current stage
ordered_attrition_contract.first_failure_assignment: assign every base scene to exactly one mutually exclusive first-failure bucket X_K, X_F, X_R, X_D, X_I, or to N_I; later-stage conditions are not evaluated after the first failure
ordered_attrition_contract.terminal_precedence: earlier stage in B->K->F->R->D->I wins; within one stage use upstream_terminal_rollup.reason_priority ascending; no scene or candidate deletion is permitted
ordered_attrition_contract.field_namespace: N_*, X_*, and their n_* aliases are bare raw numeric fields in association_attrition.csv and leaderboard denominator columns; typed leaderboard metric objects use distinct *_metric names and never overwrite these fields
ordered_attrition_contract.artifact: association_attrition.csv with one row per cell_id and all count fields, first-failure reason counts, support_id, and identity_check=PASS

cell_fatal_count_policy.scope: applies only when one non-AVAILABLE upstream status terminates the whole predictor cell before scene-specific feature evaluation
cell_fatal_count_policy.B_and_K: still execute B and the geometry/rating-join-key-only K audit over all 479 scenes without reading a rating value
cell_fatal_count_policy.F_injection: assign every K-surviving scene to X_F with the cell-fatal upstream status; set N_F=0 and retain every K failure in X_K
cell_fatal_count_policy.downstream_counts: set N_R=N_D=N_I=X_R=X_D=X_I=0; N_B=N_K+X_K, N_K=X_F, and every registered count identity must still pass
cell_fatal_count_policy.derived_zero_support: n_informative_scenes=0, n_informative_candidates=0, both pooled distinct counts=0, both raw weight sums=0.0, and support_id is SHA256 of the zero-byte payload
cell_fatal_count_policy.row_status: emit the cell-fatal rollup status/reason for all three association rows while retaining the exact raw attrition counts; no typed raw-count sentinel or invented terminal status is permitted

statistic_kernel_contract.numeric_domain: finite IEEE-754 binary64 only; normalize -0.0 to +0.0 on input and output

input_identity_and_order.ordinary_and_deletion_subsets: row identity is (segment_id,candidate_ordinal); order by segment_id raw UTF-8 bytes ascending, then candidate_ordinal integer ascending 1,2,3
input_identity_and_order.bootstrap_occurrences: row identity is (replicate_index,draw_position,source_segment_id,candidate_ordinal); within one replicate order by draw_position integer ascending 0..N_I-1, then candidate_ordinal ascending 1,2,3; source_segment_id remains payload identity and repeated source scenes are never collapsed or resorted by source_segment_id
input_identity_and_order.input_index_for_midrank_ties: zero-based position in the applicable canonical order above

terminal_status_namespace.normalization_forbidden: INELIGIBLE_ASSOCIATION_CONSTANT and NUMERICAL_FAILURE are ledger-status labels only and must never be returned as upstream association statuses

midrank.algorithm: stable-sort (value,input_index) by numeric value ascending then input_index ascending; scan maximal groups whose binary64 values compare exactly equal; for one-based occupied rank positions lo..hi assign every group member (lo+hi)/2.0; restore input order
midrank.ties: exact equality only; no tolerance, jitter, randomization, ordinal preference, or string conversion

weighted_pearson.sum_weights: sw=math.fsum(weights in input order); require finite sw>0
weighted_pearson.means: mx=math.fsum(w*x in input order)/sw and my=math.fsum(w*y in input order)/sw
weighted_pearson.centered_sums: sxy=math.fsum(w*(x-mx)*(y-my) in input order), sxx=math.fsum(w*(x-mx)**2 in input order), syy analogously
weighted_pearson.constant: if sxx<=0 or syy<=0 return ASSOCIATION_CONSTANT
weighted_pearson.result: r=sxy/math.sqrt(sxx*syy); require finite or return ASSOCIATION_NUMERICAL_FAILURE; if -1-1e-15<=r<-1 set r=-1, if 1<r<=1+1e-15 set r=1, if outside [-1-1e-15,1+1e-15] return ASSOCIATION_NUMERICAL_FAILURE; normalize -0.0 to +0.0

method_reductions.RWS: within each scene occurrence midrank its three ratings and deviations, call weighted_pearson with weights [1/3,1/3,1/3], then math.fsum(scene_rho in the applicable canonical ordinary segment order or bootstrap draw_position occurrence order)/N_I
method_reductions.PSP: midrank all 3*N_I pooled ratings and deviations in canonical input order, then weighted_pearson with 3*N_I identical weights 1/(3*N_I)
method_reductions.PPR: call weighted_pearson on raw ratings and deviations in canonical input order with 3*N_I identical weights 1/(3*N_I)

statistic_kernel_contract.subset_recomputation: fold, LOO, LOCO, shard, and common-support statistics rerun the identical kernel from ordinary canonical subset rows; bootstrap statistics rerun it from canonical draw-position occurrence rows, preserving duplicate occurrences; subtractive shortcuts, duplicate collapse, source-segment resorting of bootstrap occurrences, and cached pooled ranks are forbidden

association_axis.method: within-scene rating and deviation average midranks, Pearson correlation of the centered three-candidate rank vectors, then equal-scene arithmetic mean
association_axis.constant_policy: a constant within-scene rating or deviation vector excludes that scene from the shared mask before any association is computed

association_axis.method: Pearson correlation of pooled average-midranks of score and deviation over the exact shared 3*n candidate rows
association_axis.point_estimate: weighted Pearson with candidate_weight=1/(3*n_informative_scenes), algebraically identical to unweighted pooled Spearman here
association_axis.constant_policy: if either pooled rank vector is constant, upstream terminal status is ASSOCIATION_CONSTANT for all three association rows of that predictor cell

association_axis.method: Pearson correlation of raw finite human score and deviation over the exact shared 3*n candidate rows
association_axis.point_estimate: weighted Pearson with candidate_weight=1/(3*n_informative_scenes), algebraically identical to unweighted pooled Pearson here
association_axis.constant_policy: if either pooled raw vector is constant, upstream terminal status is ASSOCIATION_CONSTANT for all three association rows of that predictor cell

association_uncertainty.role: reported support only; never a recovery gate or ranking key
association_uncertainty.bootstrap_unit: scene with its intact three-candidate score/deviation vectors

canonical_seed_object.serialization: sorted-key compact canonical JSON plus exactly one terminal LF, UTF-8 without BOM, allow_nan=False

association_uncertainty.row_seed: int.from_bytes(SHA256(ASCII 'RQ014-ASSOCBOOT|' concatenated with canonical seed JSON UTF-8-LF).digest()[0:8],'big',signed=False)
association_uncertainty.scene_source_order: segment_id raw UTF-8 bytes ascending before assigning positions 0..N_I-1
association_uncertainty.draw_rule: for replicate_index 0..1999 call rng.integers(0,N_I,size=N_I,dtype=numpy.int64,endpoint=False) exactly once; preserve draw order and the intact three-candidate vector; no discarded or parallel draws
association_uncertainty.occurrence_identity: the j-th returned index creates occurrence (replicate_index,j,source_segment_id) with draw_position=j; pass rows to the statistic kernel in draw_position then candidate_ordinal order and never collapse or re-sort repeated source_segment_id occurrences
association_uncertainty.statistic_recomputation: rerun the exact registered RWS/PSP/PPR kernel on the drawn scene occurrences, including pooled midranks for PSP
association_uncertainty.interval: sort 2000 finite estimates ascending; for q in {0.025,0.975} set h=(2000-1)*q, j=floor(h), g=h-j, and return (1-g)*x[j]+g*x[j+1] (zero-based type-7 linear quantile)
association_uncertainty.fixture_and_hash_gate: canonical seed, first PCG64 draws, each method's resample reduction, implementation, Python executable, and environment SHA-256 fixtures must pass
association_uncertainty.failure: UNCERTAINTY_UNAVAILABLE; point estimate remains observed but robustness label is unavailable

upstream_terminal_rollup.rule: every granular terminal status is listed exactly once and maps to one immutable (ledger_status,reason_code) pair; duplicate, missing, or runtime-invented mappings are FATAL_STATUS_ROLLUP_ERROR
upstream_terminal_rollup.completeness_identity: set(rows.upstream_status) must equal the v3 rating-blind feature terminal statuses declared here union set(recovery_internal_upstream_statuses); subset-only validation is forbidden
upstream_terminal_rollup.scope_rule: scene-local occurrences populate the ordered attrition first-failure counts; when a condition terminates the whole predictor/association row, the same mapping supplies its single ledger status and reason_code
upstream_terminal_rollup.terminal_selection: within one scene select its first failed attrition stage; if multiple granular reasons exist at that stage choose smallest reason_priority then reason_code raw UTF-8 ascending; AVAILABLE and CONTINUE are never written as terminal ledger statuses
row_outcome_algorithm: if a cell-fatal upstream execution status is not AVAILABLE, apply ordered_attrition_contract.cell_fatal_count_policy, then apply its unique rollup without reading a rating value
otherwise run ordered attrition; if N_I=0 choose the earliest stage K,F,R,D,I with X_stage>0, then the smallest reason_priority and reason_code among scenes first failing there
otherwise run the registered association kernel; finite output maps ASSOCIATION_AVAILABLE to OBSERVED even when stability fields are typed NA
association constant or numerical failure maps through its unique I-stage row
exactly one terminal ledger row is emitted for every leaderboard_id

field_rules.cell_id: one of the 320 canonical registered predictor cell IDs
field_rules.first_failure_stage: exact K, F, R, D, or I from the selected rollup row; observed ASSOCIATION_AVAILABLE rows use I
field_rules.reason_priority: nonnegative JSON integer copied from the unique rollup row
field_rules.raw_denominators: exact bare numeric/digest fields defined by association_support_contract; no typed wrappers
field_rules.metrics: exact typed objects/arrays defined by stability.row_metric_fields
field_rules.rank_sort_tuple: exact 11-element array materialized from ranking.typed_sort_projection in lexicographic_key order; final element is leaderboard_id as a JSON string

append_only_ledger.canonical_json: CPython 3.9 json.dumps(row,sort_keys=True,separators=(',',':'),ensure_ascii=False,allow_nan=False) using CPython 3.9 finite-float repr, UTF-8 without BOM, followed by exactly one LF byte
append_only_ledger.hash_domain_prefix: ASCII bytes RQ014-RECOVERY-LEDGER-v2 followed by one NUL byte 0x00
append_only_ledger.record_sha256_preimage: hash_domain_prefix concatenated with canonical_json bytes of the exact row after removing only record_sha256; prev_record_sha256 remains in the preimage; no other key is omitted and the preimage retains its terminal LF
append_only_ledger.record_sha256_rule: lowercase hex SHA256(record_sha256_preimage); add this value as record_sha256, then serialize the full stored row once with canonical_json
append_only_ledger.stored_jsonl: concatenate the 960 full stored-row canonical_json byte strings in row_index order; every row including the last has exactly one LF and there are no blank lines
append_only_ledger.base_row_order: enumerate sampling_axis order, temporal_axis.recipes order, horizon_axis order, readout_axis order to obtain 320 cell_ids; within each cell enumerate association_axis order RWS, PSP, PPR; assign row_index 0..959
append_only_ledger.worker_completion_order: workers never append; the lead validates and buffers all terminal rows, then writes only base_row_order, so scheduling or completion order cannot affect bytes
append_only_ledger.hash_chain: row 0 uses genesis_prev_record_sha256; each later row prev_record_sha256 equals the immediately preceding stored row's record_sha256
append_only_ledger.partial_leaderboard_visibility: forbidden until all 960 rows are terminal

FINITE_FLOAT.value: finite JSON binary64 number with -0.0 normalized to 0.0

row_metric_fields.association_value: FINITE_FLOAT for OBSERVED and NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED) otherwise
row_metric_fields.n_informative_scenes_metric: FINITE_INT copied from the separate raw n_informative_scenes field for OBSERVED and NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED) otherwise
row_metric_fields.fold_associations: length-5 array of FINITE_FLOAT or one NA reason object for the entire unavailable vector
row_metric_fields.fold_negative_count: FINITE_INT only when all five fold values are finite, otherwise the same typed NA reason
row_metric_fields.median_fold_association: FINITE_FLOAT only when all five fold values are finite, otherwise the same typed NA reason
row_metric_fields.scenario_cluster_na_scene_count_metric: FINITE_INT count over the native shared support for OBSERVED and NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED) otherwise
row_metric_fields.eligible_cluster_count_metric: FINITE_INT for OBSERVED even when fewer than two clusters are eligible; NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED) otherwise
row_metric_fields.negative_cluster_count_metric: FINITE_INT only when every eligible cluster omission is finite, otherwise NA(NONFINITE_CLUSTER_RECOMPUTATION); NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED) for a nonobserved row
row_metric_fields.eligible_shard_count_metric: FINITE_INT for OBSERVED even when fewer than two shards are eligible; NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED) otherwise
row_metric_fields.negative_shard_count_metric: FINITE_INT only when every eligible shard omission is finite, otherwise NA(NONFINITE_SHARD_RECOMPUTATION); NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED) for a nonobserved row
row_metric_fields.association_ci_lower: FINITE_FLOAT when all 2000 association bootstrap replicates are finite, NA(UNCERTAINTY_UNAVAILABLE) for an observed row otherwise, and NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED) for a nonobserved row
row_metric_fields.association_ci_upper: FINITE_FLOAT when all 2000 association bootstrap replicates are finite, NA(UNCERTAINTY_UNAVAILABLE) for an observed row otherwise, and NA(ROW_NOT_OBSERVED) for a nonobserved row

five_fold_rule.segment_bytes: segment_id encoded once as strict UTF-8 with no BOM, normalization, length prefix, terminator, or LF

stability.fold_role: for each association method, recompute that exact method independently on the shared included scenes assigned to each fold; never average candidate rows across folds and never use folds as train/test
stability.fold_validity: all five folds must each meet the minimum and the method-specific nonconstant rule; otherwise fold_associations, fold_negative_count, and median_fold_association use the registered typed NA reason and recovery_compatible=false
stability.fold_negative_definition: finite association strictly less than 0; denominator is exactly 5
stability.leave_one_scene_out: for every shared included scene remove its intact three-candidate vector and recompute the exact association method, including pooled reranking for PSP; every deletion must be finite
stability.leave_one_scenario_cluster_out: from the native shared support count exact scenario_cluster=NA scenes separately and never treat NA as a cluster; for each eligible non-NA scenario cluster remove every scene in that cluster and recompute the exact association method on the remaining shared mask
stability.eligible_cluster_definition: a distinct non-NA scenario_cluster with at least 5 included scenes and at least 5 included scenes remaining after its removal; order eligible cluster IDs by raw UTF-8 bytes
stability.leave_one_cluster_denominator: eligible_cluster_count_metric.value; it is recorded even when 0 or 1
stability.leave_one_cluster_negative_definition: negative_cluster_count_metric is the number of eligible-cluster omission associations strictly less than 0, only when every eligible omission is finite; leave_one_cluster_out_negative_fraction equals negative_cluster_count_metric.value/eligible_cluster_count_metric.value only when eligible_cluster_count_metric.value>=2 and every omission is finite, otherwise typed NA(INSUFFICIENT_CLUSTER_SUPPORT or NONFINITE_CLUSTER_RECOMPUTATION)
stability.source_shard_leave_one_out: use checksum-bound source_shard_id; a shard is eligible with at least 5 included scenes and at least 5 remaining scenes, then remove the shard and recompute the exact association method
stability.eligible_shard_definition: a distinct checksum-bound nonmissing source_shard_id with at least 5 included scenes and at least 5 included scenes remaining after its removal; order eligible shard IDs by raw UTF-8 bytes
stability.leave_one_shard_denominator: eligible_shard_count_metric.value; it is recorded even when 0 or 1
stability.leave_one_shard_negative_definition: negative_shard_count_metric is the number of eligible-shard omission associations strictly less than 0, only when every eligible omission is finite; leave_one_shard_out_negative_fraction equals negative_shard_count_metric.value/eligible_shard_count_metric.value only when eligible_shard_count_metric.value>=2 and every omission is finite, otherwise typed NA(INSUFFICIENT_SHARD_SUPPORT or NONFINITE_SHARD_RECOMPUTATION)
stability.support_reuse_rule: all fold/LOO/LOCO/shard computations start from the same association_support_contract mask and may only remove whole scenes
stability.observed_with_stability_na: permitted; ledger_status remains OBSERVED, every unavailable metric uses a typed NA object, recovery_compatible is false, and ranking uses the explicit typed sort projection

recovery_compatible_marker.interpretation: operational compatibility with the remembered strong negative result, not a hypothesis test

accepted_residuals.STAT4-m01: scenario_cluster=NA scenes remain in native support but are not a LOCO cluster; no NA-share ceiling or leave-all-NA omission is added to recovery_compatible in this v2 contract

typed_sort_projection.recovery_compatible_rank: 0 for true, 1 for false; any required typed NA forces false
typed_sort_projection.association_finite: [0,association_value.value] after requiring exact keys {kind,value}, kind=FINITE_FLOAT, and finite value
typed_sort_projection.fold_negative_count_finite_descending: [0,-fold_negative_count.value] after requiring exact keys {kind,value}, kind=FINITE_INT, and integer value
typed_sort_projection.cluster_fraction_finite_descending: [0,-leave_one_cluster_out_negative_fraction.value] after requiring exact keys {kind,value}, kind=FINITE_FLOAT, and finite value
typed_sort_projection.n_informative_scenes_finite_descending: [0,-n_informative_scenes_metric.value] after requiring exact keys {kind,value}, kind=FINITE_INT, and integer value equal to raw n_informative_scenes

ranking.missing_sort_values: typed NA values are projected only through their registered [1,zero] tagged tuple; JSON null, NaN, infinity, and ad-hoc numeric sentinels are forbidden
ranking.float_comparison: compare finite IEEE-754 binary64 values exactly after signed-zero normalization; no epsilon grouping
ranking.support_comparability: RWS/PSP/PPR rows within a cell are exactly support-matched; cells may have frozen varying support, so rank is explicitly an operational specification-recovery order and every row carries support_id and denominators
ranking.unique_rank_rule: materialize the exact typed sort tuple, then stable lexicographic sort over all 960 unique leaderboard IDs; assign integer ranks 1..960 exactly once; raw UTF-8 leaderboard_id bytes are the final total-order key
ranking.freeze_candidate_rule: only when at least one row has ledger_status=OBSERVED and recovery_compatible=true; because status_rank and recovery_compatible_rank precede every other key, rank 1 is necessarily such a row whenever one exists

same_recipe_robustness_requirement.robust_internal_label_requires_RWS_twin: when rank 1 uses PSP or PPR, RWS for the same predictor cell and exact shared support must be negative and reported with scene-cluster uncertainty
same_recipe_robustness_requirement.failure_effect: record AGGREGATION_SENSITIVE as a separate diagnostic flag without changing the selected recipe or final same-dataset recovery verdict; never choose another recipe

adaptive_recovery_extension.base_ledger_rule: the current lane contains exactly 320 predictor cells and 960 association rows; no new cell, row, leaderboard ID, rank, or hash-chain record may be appended
adaptive_recovery_extension.no_compatible_base_row: terminate as NOT_RECOVERED_WITHIN_FROZEN_RECOVERY_GRID; do not freeze a recipe and do not replay
adaptive_recovery_extension.future_reconsideration: requires a new checksum-bound amendment, new review manifest, fresh formal review, new authorization, and a separate ledger namespace; it cannot mutate or rerank this v3 ledger

selected_recipe_freeze.creation_prerequisite: at least one of the fixed 960 rows has ledger_status=OBSERVED and recovery_compatible=true
selected_recipe_freeze.generated_mechanically_from: terminal fixed-960 leaderboard rank 1 after creation_prerequisite passes; rank 1 must itself be OBSERVED and recovery_compatible=true
selected_recipe_freeze.no_artifact_action: do not create, truncate, or replace selected_recovery_recipe.json and do not authorize clean replay
required_bindings: cell and association IDs plus rank key
sampling, resampling, temporal, horizon, frozen-M3, deviation, and aggregation contracts
estimator tree, code, environment, preprocessing, and source hashes
candidate, counterpart, WOD path-type, and frozen-M3 manifests
rating-access receipt and frozen analysis-manifest hashes
observed N, association, fold, leave-one-cluster, leave-one-shard, and leave-one-scene metrics

frozen_m3_binding.source: the exact scorer, manifest, and feature-spec artifacts declared by rating_blind_feature_bank.frozen_envelope

scene_anchor_domain_binding.selected_group_projection: from checksum-verified wod_scene_anchor_domain.csv retain the header and every row whose feature_id and horizon_id equal the selected recipe, preserving exact artifact row order and encoding; selected_group_projection_sha256 hashes those exact UTF-8 LF bytes
scene_anchor_domain_binding.replay_use: clean replay receives only the expected projection SHA/count through selected_recovery_recipe.json, independently rederives the selected per-scene groups, serializes the same projection, and requires exact count/SHA equality without reading the screen artifact

clean_independent_replay.independence: fresh agent, separate managed run root, exact published-commit closed code snapshot, and no access to the screen leaderboard except selected_recovery_recipe.json; Git worktree execution is forbidden
allowed_inputs: exact frozen M3 scorer, manifest, and feature-spec artifacts
clean_independent_replay.frozen_envelope_rule: load the exact selected_recipe_freeze.frozen_m3_binding model bytes and verify all path/size/SHA-256 fields; the envelope model is the sole non-reimplemented scientific component
clean_independent_replay.independent_rewrite_boundary: independently rewrite resampling, exact-window state and M3 feature assembly, deviation/readout reduction, rating join, and association code; do not read screen caches or screen statistic code
clean_independent_replay.scene_anchor_domain_verification: require selected_recipe_freeze.scene_anchor_domain_binding and exact independently rederived selected_group_projection_row_count/SHA-256 equality; the screen artifact itself remains forbidden input

pass_tolerances.scene_anchor_domain_group_status_reason_: exact for every segment_id x selected feature_id x selected horizon_id group

rank_vectors_by_association.RWS: exact scene-local rating and deviation midrank vectors in canonical scene/candidate order
rank_vectors_by_association.PSP: exact pooled rating and deviation midrank vectors in canonical candidate-row order
rank_vectors_by_association.PPR: NOT_APPLICABLE; PPR consumes raw vectors and no diagnostic rank vector is a replay gate

clean_independent_replay.failure_rule: debug and rerun the same frozen top recipe; never fall through to rank 2

current_structural_gate.role: G2R rating-blind candidate/future-leakage fixture and fatal gate only
current_structural_gate.verdict_effect: none after its structural gate passes; a gate failure aborts G2R before rating access

registered_controls_and_robustness.final_verdict_rule: no current control, optional envelope, or supporting statistic changes the fixed-grid selection, replay gate, or same-dataset recovery label

state_conditions.all_predictor_cells_ineligible: zero of 960 rows has ledger_status=OBSERVED; selected_recovery_recipe.json is absent and replay is forbidden
state_conditions.eligible_but_none_compatible: at least one row has ledger_status=OBSERVED and zero rows have recovery_compatible=true; selected_recovery_recipe.json is absent and replay is forbidden
state_conditions.top_compatible_not_replayed: at least one row is OBSERVED and recovery_compatible=true, rank 1 is mechanically frozen, and clean replay has not passed
state_conditions.clean_replay_mismatch: the one frozen rank-1 compatible recipe fails an exact registered replay gate; never fall through to another rank
state_conditions.clean_replay_pass: the one frozen rank-1 compatible recipe passes all replay gates and preserves recovery_compatible=true

verdict_ladder.stability_basis: the selected row and clean replay must preserve recovery_compatible=true; optional/current controls do not alter this label

staged_rating_access.G2R: rating-blind 320-cell feature bank, masks, frozen M3 envelope, the NC_PRETSTAR_HISTORY_ONLY structural leakage fixture/gate, and manifests; no control association row
staged_rating_access.G3R: single separately authorized full-rated479 rating join and 960-row recovery screen; no sealed confirmation claim
staged_rating_access.G4R: frozen selected-recipe clean replay on the same ratings
staged_rating_access.G5_NEW_DATA: optional true confirmation on ratings never used for recovery

optional_prospective_validation.legacy_v1p3_power_and_confirmation_contr: retained only as provenance, post-selection split stability, and future new-sample planning
